audit_items.proof_trace: lru_crawler_release_client has no internal allocations and no conditional branches — it unconditionally calls free(c->buf) and redispatch_conn(c->c). Full path coverage confirms: on write-error paths lru_crawler_close_client is called first (frees c->buf, sets c->c=NULL), so the guard at line 624 correctly skips lru_crawler_release_client with no leak; on malloc-failure in lru_crawler_set_client, c->buf remains NULL so free(NULL) is a safe no-op. The single heap allocation (c->buf) is freed on every reachable path.
